key_themes: Strong recurring revenue (~80% of total) and da Vinci/Ion procedure growth
Moderating 2026 guidance (13-15% procedure growth vs 18% in 2025)
High valuation with recent analyst price target cuts but maintained Buy ratings
Long-term tailwinds from aging population, low penetration, da Vinci 5 adoption, and global expansion
summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) shows cautiously positive sentiment ahead of its Q1 2026 earnings on April 21. The company delivered strong Q4 2025 results with revenue of ~$2.87B (+19% YoY), EPS of $2.53 (beat), and 18% procedure growth, driven by high-margin recurring revenue and da Vinci 5 adoption.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ISRG/earnings/ISRG-Q4-2025-earnings_call-400878.html/)[[2]](https://isrg.intuitive.com/news-releases/news-release-details/intuitive-announces-fourth-quarter-earnings-5/) However, the 13-15% procedure growth guidance for 2026 was viewed as lackluster by some, contributing to stock volatility, recent pullbacks from highs, and several analyst price target reductions (e.g. Truist to $580, others similarly lowered but ratings largely stayed Buy with average PT ~$590).[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ISRG/)[[4]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/ISRG)
sources: 20+ web/news results (Yahoo Finance, company releases, Seeking Alpha, IBD, TipRanks), Analyst coverage from 20+ firms (recent PT cuts by Truist, Mizuho, BTIG, Baird while keeping Buy/Outperform), 15+ recent X posts (primarily earnings buzz; limited deep sentiment threads), Investor forums (StockTwits, InvestorsHub) showing active discussion on valuation vs growth
